mnemonics: DP FTW: Dynamic Programming For The Win
keyPoints: Huffman Coding is a greedy algorithm that builds an optimal prefix code for lossless data compression.
The algorithm uses a priority queue (min-heap) to build a binary tree, merging the two least frequent nodes at each step.
Optimal solutions often require choosing between greedy approaches (locally optimal choices) and dynamic programming (solving subproblems).
commonMistakes: Assuming a greedy approach always yields an optimal solution (it doesn't for problems like the 0-1 Knapsack).
Confusing Huffman Coding with other compression algorithms like LZW or Run-Length Encoding.
Forgetting to consider time/space complexity trade-offs when selecting an optimal data structure for an algorithm.
